sourceMovie.title: Class
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Class works as a comedy movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The comedy and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Graduate
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Class clicked for you, The Graduate (1967) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and drama, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Class?
faq.answer: Try The Graduate, Cyrus, My Awkward Sexual Adventure—each shares DNA with Class while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Class?
faq.answer: Class (1983) is best described as Comedy, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Class worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.0/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this comedy lane.
